Matthew Chiu
Blogroll Worship Substack

Welcome, fellow traveller

Thank you for visiting my small corner of the Internet world.

I am a student of philosophy, politics and economics at the London School of Economics.

I enjoy writing and thinking about economics, cities, and societal systems. I share my occasional pontificaitons about the world on Substack.

More generally, this website is a disparate collection of my thoughts, notes, and weakly-held opinions. I hope you find them interesting.

My work

I am currently working on research in AI governance and development economics. More generally, my interests are wide-ranging and include the economics of innovation, economic growth, development economics, labour allocation, and industrial organisation.

As a student of the social sciences and humanities, I have been blessed with the opportunity to think deeply about many problems. Amongst other topics, I have written (read: speculated) about:

  • the infamous Sleeping Beauty problem in formal epistemology (I’m a thirder!)
  • why Karl Marx’s “dictatorship of the proletariat”, a transitionary state prior to communism, could be viable
  • how sovereign wealth funds can curtail the resource curse
  • the decline of Schumpeterian creative destruction for explaining low GDP growth

Most recently, I have been thinking about how the systems that that shape us — the meritocratic ideal, the democratic spirit, the educational system, and the idol of prestige — came to be.

I previously worked in management consulting, venture capital and talent investing, and early-stage startups.

Contact me

Contact: Twitter, Linkedin.

I love speaking and meeting people from all walks of life. Please, if you think I could be helpful, want to learn more about my work, or just chat casually about life and more — meet with me!

Matthew Chiu. Last updated: December 22, 2023.
